WWN.DeleteContent: Are you sure you want to delete {name} from the actor {actor}?
WWN.dialog.partySelectHint: Drag and drop characters to add them to the party.
WWN.details.name: Name
WWN.details.treasureTableHint: Drop a rollable table here to roll the monster treasure
WWN.details.instinctTableHint: Drop a rollable table here to roll an Instinctive Action
WWN.HitDiceFormula: Enter class formula for Hit Dice, as of level 1. (e.g. '1d6+2' for a full warrior)
WWN.InjuriesWoundsHint: Injuries and Fatal Wounds. Hover over each to see their effects.
WWN.InjuriesHint: Injuries. Damage in excess of your HP, along with your current injuries, are added to a die roll to determine the severity of your next injury.
WWN.WoundsHint: Fatal Wounds. You have 3 rounds in which to remove all of your fatal wounds or perish.
WWN.AB: Attack Bonus, added to all attack rolls.
WWN.DB: Damage Bonus, added to all damage rolls and shock values.
WWN.NpcConfigHint: These values apply directly to this monster. Active Effects are optional and stack on top.
WWN.LanguagesHint: Enter a list of languages, separated by commas.
WWN.Setting.InitiativeHint: Roll initiative per combatant or per side.
WWN.Setting.CollapseSidesInGroupInitiati: When using group (side) initiative, show one collapsible row per side on the combat tracker and advance Next/Previous by whole side. Turn off to list every combatant and advance one at a time.
WWN.Setting.RerollInitiativeHint: What happens to initiative at the end of each round.
WWN.Setting.MovementRateHint: Base movement table for automatic calculation.
WWN.Setting.RandomHPHint: Roll hit dice for unlinked NPC tokens when added to combat.
WWN.Setting.showMovementHint: Display a per-turn movement rate, 3x a character's encounter movement.
WWN.Setting.SignificantTreasureHint: Weight at which treasure will reduce the movement, only useful for basic encumbrance
WWN.Setting.xpConfigHint: Set how much experience characters require per level.
WWN.Setting.xpCustomListHint: Enter a list of XP values, separated by commas.
WWN.Setting.hideInstinctHint: If selected, automatic rolls on the Instinct table will be hidden from players.
WWN.items.PersonalTreasureHint: Personal treasure value in silver pieces.
WWN.items.ShockAC: The AC value under which the weapon inflicts Shock damage. Leave blank for Foci that inflict Shock regardless.
WWN.items.ArmorBonusHint: Magical bonus to an armor's base Armor Class.
WWN.items.TraumaModHint: Modifier to the character's trauma target when this armor is equipped.
WWN.items.TraumaDieHint: Trauma die and multiplier for this weapon (e.g., 1d6/x3).
WWN.items.TraumaRatingHint: Multiplier for trauma damage (e.g., 3 for x3).
WWN.items.SkillDamageLong: Add skill level to weapon damage and Shock.
WWN.items.BurstLong: This weapon can fire in burst mode, adding +2 to attack and damage rolls.
WWN.items.TechLevelHint: Weapon tech level. 0–3 is primitive; 4+ overcomes Ironhide and advanced power-armor plating.
WWN.items.typeTrait: Type a comma separated list of weapon traits: 'Melee,R,2H,Missile (10' / 20')' and press ENTER.
WWN.items.DecrementOnAttackHint: When checked, attacking with this weapon will decrease its charges by 1 (or by 3 if burst fire is used)
WWN.items.ChargeQuantity: Items with charges always have a quantity of 1. Multiples must be represented as separate items.
WWN.items.CurrencyHint: Use WWN coin standards or the full B/X standard.
WWN.items.total.long: Total Silver Value, including all coin and treasure items.
WWN.items.desc.SR: Slow Reload. It takes a Main Action to reload this weapon.
WWN.items.desc.2H: Two Handed. The weapon requires two hands to use in combat. Ranged two-handed weapons cannot be fired effectively while an enemy is within melee range.
WWN.items.desc.AP: Armor Piercing. This weapon ignores non-magical hides, armor and shields for purposes of its hit rolls.
WWN.items.desc.FX: Fixed. The weapon is too heavy and clumsy to use without a fixed position and at least five minutes to entrench it.
WWN.items.desc.L: Long. The weapon is unusually long, allowing melee attacks to be made at targets up to 10 feet distant, even if an ally is in the way. Even so, the wielder still needs to be adjacent to a foe to count as being in melee with them for the puposes of forcing Fighting Withdrawals, disrupting large ranged weapons, or similar maneuvers.
WWN.items.desc.R: Reload. The weapon takes a Move action to reload. If the user has at least Shoot-1 skill, they can reload as an On Turn action instead.
WWN.items.desc.LL: Less Lethal. Foes brought to zero hit points by this weapon can always be left alive at the wielder's discretion.
WWN.items.desc.N: Numerous. Five of these count as only one Readied item.
WWN.items.desc.PM: Precisely Murderous. When used for an Execution Attack, the weapon applies an additional -1 penalty to the Physical save and does double damage even if it succeeds.
WWN.items.desc.S: Subtle. Can be easily hidden in ordinary clothing or concealed in jewelry.
WWN.items.desc.SS: Single Shot. This weapon takes ten rounds to reload, and the reloading effort is spoiled if an enemy melees the wielder.
WWN.items.desc.T: Throwable. While the weapon can be used in melee, it may be thrown out to the listed range as well, albeit it does no Shock in that case. Throwing a weapon while a foe is in melee range applies a -4 penalty to the attack roll.
WWN.items.desc.CB: Crossbow. Due to the simplicity of their operation, someone without Shoot-0 may use a crossbow at no unskilled penalty.
WWN.spells.TimeHint: Length of time effort is committed
WWN.reaction.Hostile: {name} is as aggressively hostile as the situation allows.
WWN.reaction.Unfriendly: {name} is more unfriendly and hostile than they'd be expected to be in the given situation.
WWN.reaction.Neutral: {name} is as predictably hostile or friendly as they'd usually be in this situation.
WWN.reaction.Indifferent: {name} is more friendly and benign than you'd expect them to be, given the circumstances.
WWN.reaction.Friendly: {name} is as friendly and helpful as their nature and the situation permits them to be.

WWN.faction.attackSpecialDesc: Text (with optional inline roll) to show when the asset attacks and counters.
WWN.faction.locationRollDesc: Text (with optional inline roll) to show when the asset is at the same location as attacking or countering asset.
WWN.faction.turnRollDesc: Text (with optional inline roll) to show when the faction has a new turn. This can be for rolls or reminders. By default hidden under the 'long desc'
WWN.faction.abandonGoal: Abandon current goal {name}? Will lose income and no actions this turn.
WWN.faction.action-desc: The following list of actions includes the most common activities a faction might choose to perform. The GM should feel free to add new action types, or allow alternate choices if a faction wishes to do something that doesn't quite fit the list below. As a rule, a faction should only be able to do one thing per turn, but should be able to do that same thing with any or all assets.

WWN.Chat.NoTokenSelected: Select or target at least one token first.
WWN.ClassEdge.NoneOnDetails: None — add Classes & Edges on the Powers tab
WWN.ClassEdge.ResourceNameHint: Pool name powers draw from; use distinct names to disambiguate (e.g. Effort, Psychic Effort)
WWN.ClassEdge.FormulaInvalid: Formula could not be evaluated against this actor.
WWN.ClassEdge.ProgressionHint: Comma-separated slot maximums by character level, e.g. 1, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 4
WWN.ClassEdge.LeveledProgressionHint: One row per character level; columns are slots per spell level, e.g. "2, 1" = two L1 and one L2 slot.
WWN.ClassEdge.AttackProgressionHint: How this class contributes to the PC attack bonus at character level.
WWN.ClassEdge.SkillPointsPerLevelHint: Granted when the owning character levels up; the highest value among all Classes/Edges applies
WWN.ClassEdge.HdPerLevelModHint: Added to each hit die before CON (e.g. +2 for Warrior, −1 for Full High Mage)
WWN.ClassEdge.PreparedProgressionHint: Comma-separated prepared spell maximums by character level, e.g. 3, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12
WWN.ClassAssignment.Title: Choose Class
WWN.ClassAssignment.Hint: Select the Full and/or Partial classes for this character. Adventurers pick two Partial classes.
WWN.Currency.MultiplierHint: How many units of the base currency one unit of this denomination is worth.
WWN.Currency.CarriedHint: Quantity currently carried by this actor.
WWN.Currency.BankedHint: Quantity held in a bank or other secure storage, not counted for encumbrance.
WWN.Currency.PerSlotHint: 0 means weightless (e.g. digital currency). Carried coins of all weighted currencies are summed to determine slots used.
WWN.Currency.NoCurrencyItems: {name} has no currency items to adjust.
WWN.Dialog.ChargeHint: Applies +2 to hit and -2 AC until your next turn.
WWN.Dialog.GenerateScoresHint: Apply these 3d6-in-order results to the character?
WWN.Effects.TreatAllMeleeAsAcTen: Melee Shock Treats Targets as AC 10
WWN.Effects.MeleeCountsAsTl4: Melee / Thrown Count as TL4 vs Armor
WWN.Effects.MissAfterFirstMeleeHit: Further Melee Attacks Miss After First Hit
WWN.Focus.SparkPoolDialogTitle: Spark of Brilliance — choose three skills
WWN.Focus.SparkPoolPickCount: Select exactly {pick} skills for your Spark pool.
WWN.Focus.PsychicTechniqueNotice: {focus}: if your bonus psychic skill reached level-1, pick a free level-1 technique for that discipline (GM / Powers tab).
WWN.Focus.WildPsychicNotice: {focus}: pick a psychic discipline core (level-0) or a stand-alone level-1 technique, then add it as a Power.
WWN.Effects.TransferClearChangesWarning: Changing transfer mode will clear all changes on this Active Effect. Continue?
WWN.Effects.FocusLevelHint: When set, this effect applies at this focus owned level and above (SRD L2 is usually in addition to L1). Leave blank for effects that always apply while the focus is owned.
WWN.Favorites.Title: Favorites
WWN.Favorites.Empty: Star items to add them to your favorites.
WWN.Focus.TargetNameHint: Exact resource pool name to augment, e.g. Effort or Psychic Effort
WWN.Focus.BonusSkillsListHint: Skill slugs for this focus. With Skills to Pick equal to the list length, all are granted. With Pick less than the list length, the player chooses. Leave the list empty and set Pick to 1 for open choice (Specialist, Polymath, some origins).
WWN.Focus.BonusSkillDialogBody: Choose which bonus skill this focus grants.
WWN.Focus.BonusSkillDialogBodyMulti: Choose {pick} bonus skill(s) this focus grants.
WWN.Focus.SkillBonusDialogBody: Choose which skill receives extra dice from this focus.
WWN.Focus.SkillBonusNoSkills: This character has no skill items to link.
WWN.HitDice.Label: Hit Dice
WWN.HitDice.PerLevelModHint: Applied to every level's roll, minimum 1 per level.
WWN.HitDice.RollHint: Rolls 1 die per level, each with a minimum result of 1.
WWN.Macro.OwnedOnly: You can only create macro buttons for owned items.
WWN.Macro.Missing: Could not find item {name}. It may have been deleted.
WWN.Macro.RefreshEffortNoPc: Select your PC token to refresh Committed Effort.
WWN.Macro.RefreshEffortNoFocus: This character does not have the Spirit Familiar focus.
WWN.Macro.RefreshEffortNoUses: Spirit Familiar has no daily effort refresh remaining.
WWN.Macro.RefreshEffortNoCommit: No committed Effort found to refresh on this character.
WWN.Macro.RefreshEffortSuccess: {name} refreshed 1 point of Committed Effort (Spirit Familiar).
WWN.Migration.Title: System Migration
WWN.Migration.Prompt: Your world needs migration to system version {version}. Back up your world before proceeding. Character foci and class features that are missing updated rules may be replaced from the system compendium (old copies are saved under Items). Retired Class Ability items are archived, and existing characters are prompted once to choose Class/Edge items. Migrate now?
WWN.Migration.Started: Migrating Worlds Without Number world to version {version}. Please be patient and do not close the window.
WWN.Migration.StillRunning: Still migrating Worlds Without Number… please keep this window open.
WWN.Migration.Complete: Worlds Without Number migration to version {version} complete.
WWN.Migration.ItemSyncComplete: Updated {count} focus/class feature(s) on {actors} character(s) from the system compendium. Old copies are in Items folders named "Migration Backup — …".
WWN.Migration.ItemSyncFailed: Failed to sync character foci from the system compendium. See the console (F12) for details.
WWN.Migration.ClassAbilityCleanupComplet: Archived {count} retired Class Ability item(s) on {actors} character(s). Open each character sheet to choose Full/Partial classes. Backups are under Items → "Migration Backup — …".
WWN.Npc.DamageBonusHint: Permanent bonus added to all damage and shock (not an Active Effect).
WWN.Npc.DamageBonusHalfLevelHint: Also add half hit dice, rounded up (like Killing Blow).
WWN.Pools.Title: Resource Pools
WWN.Pools.WarnInvalidFormula: Resource formula could not be evaluated.
WWN.Pools.WarnNoClassEdge: No Class/Edge supplies this pool; maximum is 0.
WWN.Pools.WarnNoSlotRow: No progression row for this class level.
WWN.Pools.WarnSpellSlotConflict: This character has both leveled and unleveled spell slot Class/Edges; spell pools cannot be resolved.
WWN.Power.CustomTypeNameHint: Display name for grouping on the actor sheet (Custom powers only)
WWN.Power.SourceHint: Tradition or class label for display (does not affect pool matching)
WWN.Power.UseLimitReached: {name} has reached its use limit for this time period.
WWN.Power.DamageRollHint: Godbound conversion applies only to this roll
WWN.Power.HealingHint: When checked, the damage roll chat card starts in healing mode.
WWN.Power.ActivationRollTypeHint: How the activation roll result is interpreted
WWN.Power.ActivationRollTargetHint: Target number when roll type is Above or Below
WWN.Power.ResourceNameHint: Must match a Class/Edge poolGrant name (e.g. Effort, Psychic Effort). Spells use Spell Slots.
WWN.Power.InternalMaxHint: Per-power use cap per time period — separate from shared pool commitment. Set to 0 for unlimited activations.
WWN.Power.UseRefreshHint: When uses of this power reset: Scene (GM Scene refresh) or Day (GM Day refresh). Separate from shared pool commitment.
WWN.Power.PreparedHint: Prepared spells can be cast; unprepared spells are blocked at use time
WWN.Power.NotPrepared: {name} is not prepared and cannot be cast.
WWN.Power.PreparedAtMax: This character has prepared the maximum number of spells.
WWN.Power.PreparedOverMax: More spells are marked prepared than the allowed maximum.
WWN.Power.PermanentStrainHint: Reduces maximum System Strain while this item is installed.
WWN.Power.UserStrainHint: Strain added to the user when the power is activated (e.g. "1" or "0,1" when strain is optional).
WWN.Power.TargetStrainHint: Strain applied to targets via a button on the power chat card.
WWN.Power.TargetStrainChoiceHint: Choose how much System Strain to apply to the selected target(s).
WWN.Power.StrainChoiceHint: Choose how much System Strain to spend.
WWN.Power.StrainFullActor: {name} does not have enough System Strain capacity remaining.
WWN.Power.PoolEmpty: The {name} pool has no remaining capacity.
WWN.Power.ActivateHint: Spend pool commitment and activate this power while it remains on
WWN.Power.DeactivateHint: Click to deactivate and reclaim committed resource
WWN.Power.EffectApplicationHint: For Scene or Day commitment: apply embedded Active Effects to the caster on use, or to selected targets via the chat card
WWN.Power.EffectsAlreadyApplied: Those effects are already active on the selected target(s)
WWN.Power.CommitmentOptionsHint: Each option with cost greater than 0 commits from the actor shared pool (e.g. 1 Effort for the Scene). Use + to add multiple options.
WWN.Power.NoCommitmentOptions: Add at least one pool commitment option.
WWN.Power.CommitmentChoiceHint: Choose how much of the shared pool to commit for this activation.
WWN.Power.CommitmentSummaryHint: Available commitment tiers for this power
WWN.Power.BonusSkillsListHint: Skill slugs granted by this power at a single rank (untrained → 0). Powers never use the focus skill-points setting.
WWN.Power.BonusSkillsModeHint: Listed uses the skill slug list. Any prompts for one primary skill when the list is empty and Skills to Pick is set.
WWN.Power.EmptyHint: Add a Class/Edge, Focus, or Power to get started.
WWN.Roll.ApplyToMaxHpHint: If the roll beats your current max HP, take it; otherwise gain +1 max HP.
WWN.Roll.HitDiceHint: Roll your full hit dice pool
WWN.Roll.HitDiceIncrement: Rolled {total}, not above {old} — maximum HP becomes {max} (+1).
WWN.Setting.Menu.Title: Worlds Without Number Settings
WWN.Setting.Menu.Appearance.Name: Appearance
WWN.Setting.Menu.Appearance.Label: Configure Appearance
WWN.Setting.Menu.Appearance.Hint: Interface theme for sheets, dialogs, and chat cards (per user).
WWN.Setting.Menu.WWN.Name: Worlds Without Number
WWN.Setting.Menu.WWN.Label: Configure WWN Rules
WWN.Setting.Menu.WWN.Hint: Core fantasy rules: combat, character progression, movement, and equipment.
WWN.Setting.Menu.CWN.Name: Cities Without Number
WWN.Setting.Menu.CWN.Label: Configure CWN Rules
WWN.Setting.Menu.CWN.Hint: Urban horror and modern combat options for Cities Without Number.
WWN.Setting.Menu.Godbound.Name: Godbound
WWN.Setting.Menu.Godbound.Label: Configure Godbound Rules
WWN.Setting.Section.AWNHint: Sci-fi options that pair with Stars Without Number play.
WWN.Setting.Section.GodboundSavesHint: Set Save Set to Godbound under Worlds Without Number → Attributes & Saving Throws.
WWN.Setting.UiThemeHint: Theme applied to sheets, dialogs, chat cards, and the interface for this user.
WWN.Setting.HideInstinctHint: Whisper instinct check results to the GM.
WWN.Setting.ShowMovementHint: Derive PC movement from encumbrance. When off, players set base movement on their sheets.
WWN.Setting.SaveSetHint: Which saving throw list and derivation the world uses.
WWN.Setting.RemoveLevelSaveHint: Do not subtract character level when deriving save targets.
WWN.Setting.SeparateRangedACHint: Track ranged AC separately (CWN-style) on sheets and attacks.
WWN.Setting.UseTraumaHint: Enable trauma dice, trauma targets, and traumatic hits.
WWN.Setting.UseAlienationHint: Show the Alienation tracker and apply cyberware alienation costs.
WWN.Setting.UseFlatArmorPenaltyHint: Apply flat Sneak/Exert penalties from armor type.
WWN.Setting.GodboundDamageHint: Convert damage rolls using the Godbound damage chart (1 or less = 0, 2-5 = 1, 6-9 = 2, 10+ = 4).
WWN.Setting.MedRangeHint: Show a medium range band on missile weapons.
WWN.Setting.XpPerCharHint: Let each character track their own XP threshold instead of a world table.
WWN.Setting.SkillSetHint: Which game line's skill list to add to new PCs and empty character sheets. Existing characters are not changed.
WWN.Setting.SkillSetPackMissing: The selected skill pack is missing or not loaded.
WWN.Setting.SkillSetEmpty: The selected skill pack has no primary skills to add.
WWN.Setting.FlatSkillCostHint: All skill levels cost 1 point instead of level + 2.
WWN.Setting.NoSkillLevelReqHint: Ignore the level requirement when buying skill levels.
WWN.Setting.BonusSkillsGrantPointsAtFirs: Foci grant skill points at level 1
If on, foci grant 3 skill points even when added at character creation, instead of simply increasing a skill by one rank. At level 2+, foci always use the skill-point path.
WWN.Setting.LanguagesHint: Comma-separated languages offered when adding a language.
WWN.Setting.DefaultCurrencySetHint: Currency items seeded onto newly created PCs.
WWN.Sheet.Name: Name
WWN.Sheet.ArmorClassHintDual: Ranged Armor Class · Melee Armor Class
WWN.Sheet.InnateAcHint: Unarmored AC floor from focus or effect (Dex and shield still apply).
WWN.Sheet.ImmuneToSurpriseHint: This character cannot be surprised or targeted with Execution Attacks (GM adjudication).
WWN.Sheet.StabilizedHint: Automatically stabilized after being reduced to 0 HP.
WWN.Starship.CombatBonusHpHint: Temporary hull from Star Captain; damage hits this pool first; clears when combat ends.
WWN.Skills.Title: Skills
WWN.Skills.UnspentHint: Available skill points. Click to edit.
WWN.Skills.PointsChangedBody: {user} changed {name}'s available skill points from {from} to {to}.
WWN.Skills.LevelIncreasedBody: {user} spent {cost} skill point(s) to increase {name}'s {skill} from {from} to {to}.
WWN.Skills.SkillUp: Buy next level with skill points
WWN.Skills.LevelIncreasedInvestedBody: {user} spent {unspent} unspent and {invested} invested skill point(s) to increase {name}'s {skill} from {from} to {to}.
WWN.Skills.LevelTooLow: Character level is too low for that skill level.
WWN.Skills.LevelUpGrantBody: {user} increased {name} to level {level}. {gained} skill point(s) added ({perLevel} per level).
WWN.Weapon.AmmoFallbackHint: If no linked ammo item is selected, match an inventory gear item whose name contains this text.
WWN.Weapon.ReloadNotMagazine: Only magazine weapons can be reloaded.
WWN.items.ExpendOnUseHint: When rolled/used from the sheet, spend one charge (or one quantity if charges are unset).
WWN.Starship.ApplyHullHint: Copy this hull's stats, budgets, and crew capacity onto the ship. Existing values are overwritten; fittings are untouched.
WWN.Starship.NoHullSelected: Select a hull before applying its preset.
WWN.Starship.UnknownHull: Unknown hull type; no preset applied.
WWN.Starship.ReassignCrewHint: Drag actors from the sidebar or between stations. Drop on a station to assign or move them.
WWN.Starship.RollComingSoon: {name} rolls are coming in a future update.
WWN.Starship.StationUnassigned: No one is crewing the {station} station.
WWN.Starship.NoStationSkill: {actor} has no {skill} skill to crew this station.
WWN.PowerArmor.ApplyFrameHint: Copy frame mass/power/cost and install integral fittings.
WWN.PowerArmor.NoFrameSelected: Select a frame before applying its preset.
WWN.PowerArmor.UnknownFrame: Unknown frame type; no preset applied.
WWN.PowerArmor.DropPilot: Drop a PC actor here to assign as pilot
WWN.PowerArmor.PilotMustBePc: Only PC actors can pilot modular power armor.
WWN.PowerArmor.AssignPilotHint: Assign a pilot (Details tab or drop a PC on this sheet) to use the character overlay.
WWN.PowerArmor.NoPilotWeapons: The pilot has no weapons to transfer.
WWN.PowerArmor.DamageNoPilot: Suit Soak absorbed damage, but overflow could not reach a pilot (none assigned).
WWN.PowerArmor.NoEmergencyCell: No unused Emergency Power Cell is installed.
WWN.Setting.UseGoldStandardHint: Use a gold standard for currency, rather than a silver standard.
WWN.Setting.ReplaceStrainWithWoundsHint: Replace System Strain with Injuries/Wounds tracking.
